commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The external draft needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and a branch update for the review finding, so this should stay in normal PR review rather than a cleanup close or automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain concern, but the new structured security-adjacent diagnostic can miss a readable config file when the state directory is missing.; Review finding: [P2] Keep chec... (sha=c39ead5c6049ff7560d8b513e179b79c15958fc9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `core/doctor/state-integrity` structured health check with issue-to-finding/effect mapping and registers it alongside the existing Doctor state-integrity contribution.

PR surface: Source +338, Tests +75. Total +413 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the PR head returns after `missing-state-dir` before the config-permission branch, while current main's legacy path checks config permissions independently. I did not run a live CLI repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The external draft needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and a branch update for the review finding, so this should stay in normal PR review rather than a cleanup close or automated repair lane.

target.title: Doctor: expose state integrity findings
target.body:
## Summary

- add structured `core/doctor/state-integrity` findings for state directory, config permission, storage-class, and runtime state directory issues
- add dry-run repair effects for the same issue set while keeping real mutations owned by the existing legacy `noteStateIntegrity` path
- keep the Doctor contribution order intact and register the new structured check under `doctor:state-integrity`

## Validation

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

- No public SDK/plugin/config contract changes; the package surface guard reports `package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0`.
- Commit used `--no-verify` because this WSL worktree reuses a symlinked dependency tree; the direct formatter/lint/type/test gates above passed.
- The existing mirror still emits stale auto-gc missing-object warnings, but branch creation, validation, commit, and push succeeded.
target.author: giodl73-repo
